decks.notes: This Rakdos Aggro deck focuses on early aggression and disruption, relying on efficient removal spells like Fatal Push and Thoughtseize to clear the way for powerful threats like Bloodtithe Harvester and Kroxa, Titan of Death's Hunger. The deck also includes versatile creatures like Mutavault and Sheoldred, the Apocalypse to maintain board presence and pressure the opponent. By combining aggressive creatures with disruptive spells, the deck aims to quickly overwhelm opponents and secure victories through a mix of direct damage and powerful creatures.

The deck focuses on assembling a combo involving Greasefang, Okiba Boss and Parhelion II to create a powerful, game-winning board state. The deck includes various support cards such as Thoughtseize for disruption, defensive creatures like Iron-Shield Elf, and removal spells like Fatal Push and Vanishing Verse to control the board. The mana base is carefully constructed to support the combo while providing the necessary colors for the spells in the deck. Overall, the deck aims to quickly assemble its combo pieces and overwhelm the opponent with a powerful synergy between its cards.

The deck revolves around assembling a powerful combo involving Greasefang and Parhelion II to quickly overwhelm the opponent. It includes disruption with cards like Thoughtseize and Fatal Push to buy time to set up the combo. The deck also utilizes protection cards like Sheltered by Ghosts and Seasoned Hallowblade to protect its key pieces. Monument to Endurance helps to fetch needed cards and maintain card advantage. Overall, the deck focuses on efficient removal and disruption while working towards the combo win condition.

The deck focuses on controlling the game through countermagic, removal, and card advantage while using Kaheera as a companion for additional value. It includes a variety of card draw and mana fixing to ensure consistency in casting spells. The main win condition is to outvalue the opponent by controlling the board and eventually overwhelming them with powerful threats like Teferi, Hero of Dominaria and Elspeth, Storm Slayer. The deck aims to disrupt the opponent's game plan while setting up its own win conditions for a long and grindy game.

This Boros Control deck focuses on utilizing a variety of removal spells, land destruction, and card draw to maintain board control and disrupt the opponent's game plan. The deck also includes cards that provide additional value in the form of card advantage and protection. The goal is to outgrind the opponent and eventually overwhelm them with powerful threats like The Wandering Emperor and Ultima, while also having answers to deal with any threats they may present.

The main concept of this deck revolves around utilizing the combo potential of Greasefang Parhelion, Monument to Endurance, and Fleeting Spirit to generate powerful board presence and win conditions. By controlling the board with removal spells like Fatal Push and Vanishing Verse, as well as disruption like Thoughtseize, the deck aims to protect its combo pieces and overwhelm the opponent. The mana base is built to support the color requirements of the deck and ensure consistent access to the necessary resources.

This UR Aggro deck focuses on quickly putting pressure on the opponent with cheap, aggressive creatures like Monastery Swiftspear and Emberheart Challenger. The deck aims to utilize cheap spells like Fiery Impulse and Reckless Rage to clear the way for its creatures to deal damage. Utilizing cards like Academic Dispute and Stormchaser's Talent helps to maintain card advantage and tempo. The deck also includes some land manipulation with Boomerang Basics and Riverpyre Verge to disrupt the opponent's board state. Overall, the deck is all about fast, efficient aggression.

The main focus of this UR Aggro deck is to maintain a fast and aggressive playstyle by utilizing cards that can deal damage quickly and efficiently. The deck includes cards that can give additional card advantage and tempo, as well as spells that can deal direct damage to the opponent's creatures or life total. The deck also includes creatures with prowess abilities to pump them up and deal extra damage. Overall, the key concept of this deck is to overwhelm the opponent and finish the game quickly with a combination of direct damage, aggressive creatures, and effective card draw.

The main focus of this deck is to ramp up mana quickly with cards like Llanowar Elves and Elvish Mystic, while utilizing powerful creatures such as Archon of Emeria and Skyclave Apparition to apply early pressure on the opponent. Thalia, Guardian of Thraben and Aven Interrupter also help disrupt the opponent's game plan, while Collected Company allows for efficient creature deployment. The deck aims to overwhelm the opponent with a fast and aggressive strategy, utilizing a mix of creatures and mana acceleration to secure victory.

This Selesnya Aggro deck focuses on aggressive creature plays and efficient mana ramp to overwhelm opponents quickly. Key cards like Archon of Emeria and Thalia, Guardian of Thraben disrupt opponents' strategies, while Collected Company provides card advantage and enables explosive turns. Skyclave Apparition deals with problematic threats, while Michelangelo's Technique and Ouroboroid offer additional reach and protection. The deck aims to establish a strong board presence early on and maintain pressure to secure a victory before opponents can stabilize.

This deck focuses on assembling the Greasefang Parhelion combo to overwhelm the opponent. It includes cards that support the combo such as Fleeting Spirit, Monument to Endurance, and Parhelion II. The deck also includes disruption with cards like Thoughtseize and Fatal Push to control the board and protect the combo pieces. The mana base is carefully constructed to ensure smooth gameplay and maximize the efficiency of the combo. Overall, the deck aims to quickly assemble the combo and overwhelm the opponent with powerful creatures and synergies.

The main concept of this Selesnya Aggro deck is to overwhelm the opponent with a fast and aggressive strategy. The deck focuses on playing low-cost creatures like Llanowar Elves and Elvish Mystic to quickly build a board presence, while also using cards like Collected Company to put more creatures onto the battlefield. Thalia, Guardian of Thraben and Archon of Emeria disrupt the opponent's game plan by taxing their spells, while Skyclave Apparition and Badgermole Cub provide removal and threats. The deck aims to maintain pressure and tempo, ultimately leading to a quick victory.

This deck focuses on controlling the game through counterspells, removal, and card advantage. It utilizes cards like Teferi, Hero of Dominaria and Supreme Verdict to control the board, while also utilizing Consult the Star Charts for card draw. The deck aims to slow down the opponent's game plan with counterspells like Dovin's Veto and Temporary Lockdown, while also utilizing cards like Stock Up for additional card advantage. The main win condition of the deck is through Kaheera, the Orphanguard and March of Otherworldly Light to finish off the opponent.

This deck focuses on using a combination of Greasefang and Parhelion II to create a powerful synergy that overwhelms the opponent. By utilizing cards like Thoughtseize and Duress to disrupt the opponent's game plan, along with removal spells like Fatal Push and Vanishing Verse to clear the way, the deck aims to set up its combo pieces while maintaining control of the board. The mana base is carefully constructed to support the deck's strategy, allowing for smooth gameplay and consistent performance in Pioneer 1 vs 1 matches.

This deck focuses on overwhelming the opponent with aggressive creatures and burn spells, applying constant pressure and dealing direct damage to the opponent's life total. The key strategy is to quickly reduce the opponent's life total to zero before they have a chance to stabilize or mount a defense. The inclusion of cards like Monastery Swiftspear and Soul-Scar Mage allow for early aggression, while cards like Burst Lightning and Reckless Rage provide efficient removal and reach. Den of the Bugbear and Ramunap Ruins serve as additional sources of damage to close out the game.

This UR Aggro deck focuses on quickly deploying low-cost creatures like Emberheart Challenger, Monastery Swiftspear, and Soul-Scar Mage to deal damage to the opponent as quickly as possible. Spells like Fiery Impulse and Reckless Rage help to clear the way for attacks, while cards like Boomerang Basics and Academic Dispute disrupt the opponent's game plan. The deck's mana base is optimized for consistent early aggression, with lands like Spirebluff Canal and Riverpyre Verge providing the necessary red and blue mana sources. Stormchaser's Talent and Heroes' Hangout provide additional reach and card advantage to keep the pressure on opponents.
